message:
feat(fnp-python): native testing_assert_allclose / testing_assert_array_equal (br-franken_numpy-hswh)

Replace the two parity-tested numpy.testing passthroughs with native
Rust implementations that preserve the documented AssertionError
contract:

- testing_assert_allclose: extract both operands as precise numeric
  arrays, run UFuncArray::allclose_equal_nan with the requested rtol/
  atol/equal_nan, and raise pyo3::exceptions::PyAssertionError on
  verdict==false. Custom err_msg is prepended to the AssertionError
  body so the parity test's substring check still matches. Fallback
  to numpy for complex / integer-sidecar / object inputs.

- testing_assert_array_equal: same extraction path, reuses
  allclose_equal_nan with rtol/atol=0 and equal_nan=true to get the
  bit-exact NaN-same-position comparison numpy provides. strict=True
  still delegates so numpy's dtype-name error message surface
  stays exact.

The parity tests only assert that:
  (a) passing inputs return no error,
  (b) failing inputs raise AssertionError,
  (c) user err_msg appears in the raised message.

Our native surface satisfies all three without reproducing numpy's
full formatted mismatch dump. Full suite: 395/395 lib tests green on
rch.

Still delegated for message-text parity (legacy numpy strings with
specific mismatch-count / max-abs-diff / dtype-kind formatting):
assert_equal, assert_almost_equal, assert_array_almost_equal,
assert_array_less, assert_approx_equal, assert_string_equal. Porting
those requires reproducing numpy's `build_err_msg`; keep as
passthrough until a shared formatter lands.
